followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I keep the same basic verdict as cycle 1. Other reviewers reinforced the central limitation: the bundle contains only an abstract and authorization-gated metadata. I add the useful point that the 'Einstein wrong' framing appears external to the paper, and I spell out more concrete systematics that a full-text reviewer should check. I do not raise the score based on venue or author reputation.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Claude's upgrade of some abstract-only claims to partially or well supported relies too much on plausibility, PRL acceptance, and the Ketterle group's reputation; those are not location-grounded evidence from the bundle.

Gemini's label of the missing full text as a fatal technical objection overstates the case: it is fatal to this bundle's reviewability, but it is not evidence that the published paper itself is wrong.

Any conclusion that the theoretical framework is already standard, or that novelty is only moderate, requires references or full-text comparison; it is plausible background knowledge but not auditable from this bundle alone.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The other reviewers correctly identified the missing full text, methods, figures, and supplemental material as the dominant barrier to technical evaluation.

Claude's point that photon collection geometry, detection efficiency, Doppler broadening, density changes, and other trapped/free systematics must be controlled is a valid human spot-check target, though not evidence of a flaw by itself.
Claude's observation that the 'Einstein wrong' framing is not supported by the title or abstract is valid and should not be counted as a paper-level claim unless the full text says otherwise.
Gemini's insistence that the main text is unavailable is valid as a reviewability objection.

main_claims.claim: The paper studies light scattering of atomic wave packets in free space using atom-photon entanglement and which-way information as the interpretive framework.
main_claims.notes: The abstract clearly states this framing, but no model, observable definitions, or derivation is present in the bundle.

main_claims.claim: The authors realize a Gedanken experiment by interfering single photons scattered from Heisenberg uncertainty-limited single-atom wave packets produced from ultracold atoms released from an optical lattice.
main_claims.notes: This is plausible as an AMO experiment, but the bundle lacks preparation details, occupancy statistics, wave-packet characterization, and photon-interference data.

main_claims.claim: Measurements before and during wave-packet expansion unify the free-space and trapped-atom pictures.
main_claims.notes: The comparison is asserted, but no matching protocol, measured quantities, or uncertainty analysis is available.

main_claims.claim: The coherence properties of scattered light are independent of the presence of the trap.
main_claims.notes: This is the strongest technical conclusion. It requires quantitative coherence measurements and systematics controls not present in the provided text.

main_claims.claim: Recoilless scattering in a trap, sideband frequency differences, and excitation of excited harmonic-oscillator states are not essential to determining coherent versus incoherent scattering fractions.
main_claims.notes: The claim may be conceptually consistent with a which-way-information account, but the regime assumptions and derivation are absent.

main_claims.claim: Atomic Mott insulators are useful for creating single-atom wave packets for fundamental studies.
main_claims.notes: The abstract states this as a capability demonstrated by the experiment. The bundle supports that this is the authors' platform, but not the quantitative quality of the realized wave packets.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A precise operational definition of coherent and incoherent scattering fractions is needed.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The central conclusion depends on what observable is called coherence and how coherent and incoherent fractions are separated experimentally.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The trapped and expanding measurements must be matched or corrected for geometry, detection efficiency, Doppler effects, density changes, and timing.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: A measured equality or difference in coherence could be produced by changing collection or atomic conditions rather than by the presence or absence of the trap.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The optical lattice/Mott-insulator preparation must yield isolated single-atom wave packets with quantified vacancy and double-occupancy rates.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Multi-atom occupancy or defects could introduce collective or ensemble scattering effects that are not the stated single-atom Gedanken experiment.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The free-expansion stage must actually realize the intended free-space regime.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Residual lattice potentials, interactions during expansion, stray fields, or finite expansion time could complicate the claimed trapped/free comparison.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The nonessentiality of recoilless scattering, sidebands, and harmonic-oscillator excitation must be stated within a definite parameter regime.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Those mechanisms may be relevant in other limits, so the conclusion needs conditions under which the which-way-information account subsumes them.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the full PRL article define and measure coherent versus incoherent scattering fractions quantitatively, with uncertainty estimates?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The provided bundle excludes the article text, figures, and supplemental material.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are trapped and free-expansion measurements matched or corrected for collection geometry, detection efficiency, Doppler broadening, density evolution, residual potentials, and timing?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: These controls would be in methods, data figures, or supplement, none of which are supplied.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the single-atom, Heisenberg-limited wave-packet preparation verified with occupation statistics and position/momentum characterization?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The abstract asserts the preparation but gives no quantitative characterization.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the derivation really show that recoilless scattering, sidebands, and harmonic-oscillator excitation are nonessential, and under what parameter assumptions?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The theoretical argument and parameter regime are absent from the bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: How accurately does the paper situate itself relative to prior Mössbauer-effect, Lamb-Dicke, sideband, and which-way-information literature?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The references and introduction are not included.

novelty_assessment: The claimed novelty is a clean experimental comparison of coherent and incoherent single-photon scattering from trapped versus freely expanding single-atom wave packets, with a conceptual unification of trap-based and free-space descriptions. That could be a worthwhile AMO/quantum-optics contribution. However, the bundle lacks the introduction and references, so claims about novelty relative to prior Mössbauer, Lamb-Dicke, sideband, and atom-photon-entanglement literature remain unaudited. I do not treat author reputation, PRL venue, or media pickup as technical evidence.
score_rationale: The abstract describes a plausible and potentially significant AMO experiment, but nearly all technical support is outside the supplied bundle. I therefore score the available evidence low on technical soundness while leaving novelty and significance moderate because the stated problem is interesting. The score is not a claim that the published PRL is wrong; it reflects that this review package is not sufficient for technical validation.
summary: The supplied bundle is not a proof-bearing mathematics manuscript; it is an APS landing-page extract for a PRL experimental AMO physics paper. The available scientific content is essentially the title, abstract, metadata, and an authorization notice. The abstract describes a potentially interesting experiment on coherent and incoherent light scattering from single-atom wave packets, but the bundle does not include the article body, figures, methods, equations, supplemental material, or references. My cycle-2 judgment remains evidence-limited: I can audit the stated claims and the other reviewers' objections, but I cannot certify the experimental or theoretical result.

technical_objections.evidence: Only abstract-level prose and journal metadata are supplied; methods, equations, figures, data, error bars, supplement, and references are absent.
technical_objections.objection: The review bundle is insufficient to audit the main technical claims.
technical_objections.paper_location: Bundle Source Text; APS page after abstract states authorization is required and lists article text, supplemental material, and references as unavailable in the extract

technical_objections.evidence: The abstract gives no measured coherence observable, no comparison figure, no statistical uncertainty, and no systematic-error discussion for trapped versus expanding wave packets.
technical_objections.objection: The trap-independence conclusion is not locally auditable from the provided text.

technical_objections.evidence: No wave-packet widths, momentum distributions, occupation statistics, uncertainty product, temperature, or lattice-site preparation fidelity are provided.
technical_objections.objection: The stated realization of Heisenberg uncertainty-limited single-atom wave packets is unsupported in the bundle.

technical_objections.evidence: The bundle does not specify Lamb-Dicke parameters, trap frequencies, recoil scale, scattering geometry, pulse duration, detection bandwidth, or the theoretical reduction connecting these effects to which-way information.
technical_objections.objection: The conclusion that Mössbauer-like recoilless scattering, sideband frequencies, and harmonic-oscillator excitation are not essential depends on unstated regime assumptions.

technical_objections.evidence: The title and abstract describe standard quantum-optics/AMO concepts and do not claim a contradiction of relativity or of an Einstein prediction.
technical_objections.objection: The media framing that the paper proves Einstein wrong is not supported by the paper text available in the bundle.
technical_objections.paper_location: Metadata comments mention a Sabine video; title and abstract do not mention Einstein

technical_objections.evidence: The title, journal, abstract, and PhySH headings identify an experimental physics article, not a theorem-proof mathematics paper.
technical_objections.objection: The paper is misclassified for this board as a mathematics paper.
technical_objections.paper_location: Metadata subjects and source text title/PhySH headings
